reasoning: The FRED tool sets the short-term probability of an increase at 0.34, driven primarily by mean reversion since the recent values (~2.55) are structurally high compared to the 6-month trailing mean (~2.2-2.3). The data is noisy and tracks high-frequency macroeconomic fluctuations, so maintaining a value >2.55 is statistically less likely in the short term. As the forecast horizon increases (1 week → 1 month → 3 months → 6 months), uncertainty increases, the distribution of future values widens, and the base predictive power of the current high value fades, driving the estimate closer to 0.5.
